page.title: Marbella Sunset Photoshoot on a Yacht

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella sunset photoshoot yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Rates from €749/2h with skipper, drinks & VAT included. Reserve now.
data.summary: Capture golden-hour portraits on a luxury yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Our fleet includes Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 from €749/2h with all inclusions.
key_facts: Charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella – sunset slot typically 18:00–20:00 in summer.
Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) from €749/2h; Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) from €749/2h.
All rates include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Larger vessels (e.g. Mangusta 80, 24m) available from €4,719/4h with a free jet ski.
Sunset shoots work best with the golden hour light: 90 minutes before sunset is prime.
Popular photo spots: Marbella coastline, La Concha backdrop, Puerto Banús harbour entrance.
Bring your own photographer or request one via WhatsApp – we can help arrange.
No hidden extras: what you see is what you pay, skipper included.

faq.q: How much does a Marbella sunset photoshoot on a yacht cost?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. For a longer session, 4 hours is €1,299 and 8 hours is €2,299. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 are available from €4,719 for 4 hours.

faq.q: What is included in the sunset photoshoot charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just bring your camera and your group.

faq.q: Can I bring my own photographer on the yacht?
faq.a: Absolutely. There is no extra charge for bringing a photographer. If you need a recommendation, let us know via WhatsApp and we can help arrange a local professional.

faq.q: What time does the sunset photoshoot start?
faq.a: In summer, sunset is around 21:00–21:30. We recommend boarding at 18:00 or 19:00 to capture the golden hour light. For winter sunsets (around 18:00), a 16:00 or 17:00 start works best. We will confirm the exact slot when you book.

faq.q: Which yacht is best for a photoshoot?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 are popular for their open flybridge and clean lines. For a larger group or a more dramatic backdrop, the Mangusta 80 (24m) offers plenty of deck space. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Can we customise the drinks or snacks for the photoshoot?
faq.a: Yes. The standard package includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. If you want champagne, cocktails, or a catering upgrade, let us know on WhatsApp and we will quote accordingly.

faq.q: How many people can join the sunset photoshoot?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 carry up to 9 and 11 passengers respectively. The Mangusta 80 can take up to 12. For smaller groups, the Speedboat (6 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are also options.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day?
faq.a: We monitor conditions closely. If the captain deems it unsafe, we will reschedule at no extra cost or offer a full refund. For light drizzle or cloud cover, the shoot can still go ahead – overcast light can be flattering for portraits.

data.body_html:
Capture the golden hour on the Costa del Sol with a Marbella sunset photoshoot yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Whether you are celebrating an engagement, a milestone birthday, or simply want professional portraits with the Mediterranean as your backdrop, our fleet offers the perfect platform. Rates start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, with skipper, drinks, and VAT included. No hidden fees, no surprises.

Why choose a yacht for your sunset photoshoot in Marbella?

Marbella's coastline runs east–west, meaning the sun sets directly over the sea from late spring to early autumn. From the deck of a yacht, you get unobstructed views of the horizon, the Sierra Blanca mountains, and the iconic La Concha peak. Unlike a beach or a rooftop, a yacht gives you complete privacy and the ability to change angles by moving a few hundred metres along the coast. The light at 20:00 in July is soft, warm, and flattering – ideal for portraits.

Our recommended yachts for a sunset photoshoot

We have two tiers that work especially well for photography. The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are both Tier A boats, priced at €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, or €2,299 for 8 hours. They feature open flybridges and clean deck lines that frame the sunset beautifully. For a larger group or a more dramatic vessel, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is available from €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. All depart from Puerto Banús.

What is included in your sunset photoshoot charter?

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You do not need to bring anything except your camera and your group. If you want to upgrade the drinks or add catering, just message us on WhatsApp and we will arrange it.

Best spots along the Marbella coast for sunset photos

Your skipper will know the prime locations depending on wind and sea conditions. Popular routes include cruising past the Golden Mile villas, anchoring near Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, or heading towards Cabopino for a more secluded backdrop. The harbour entrance at Puerto Banús itself offers a striking contrast of white superyachts against the orange sky. For a longer trip, Estepona or Sotogrande are within reach on a 4-hour charter.

How to plan your sunset photoshoot timing

Sunset time varies through the year. In June and July, the sun sets around 21:30–21:45, so boarding at 18:00 or 19:00 gives you a full 90 minutes of golden light. In September, sunset is around 20:30, so a 17:00 start works well. We will confirm the exact timing when you book. The 2-hour charter is enough for a focused shoot; the 4-hour option allows for multiple locations and a relaxed pace.

Can I bring my own photographer?

Yes, and there is no extra charge. Many of our guests bring a professional photographer or a friend with a good camera. If you need a recommendation, we can connect you with local photographers who know the best angles and lighting conditions on the water. Just ask via WhatsApp when booking.

Frequently asked questions

How much does a Marbella sunset photoshoot on a yacht cost?

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. For a longer session, 4 hours is €1,299 and 8 hours is €2,299. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 are available from €4,719 for 4 hours.

What is included in the sunset photoshoot charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just bring your camera and your group.

Can I bring my own photographer on the yacht?

Absolutely. There is no extra charge for bringing a photographer. If you need a recommendation, let us know via WhatsApp and we can help arrange a local professional.

What time does the sunset photoshoot start?

In summer, sunset is around 21:00–21:30. We recommend boarding at 18:00 or 19:00 to capture the golden hour light. For winter sunsets (around 18:00), a 16:00 or 17:00 start works best. We will confirm the exact slot when you book.

Which yacht is best for a photoshoot?

The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 are popular for their open flybridge and clean lines. For a larger group or a more dramatic backdrop, the Mangusta 80 (24m) offers plenty of deck space. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Can we customise the drinks or snacks for the photoshoot?

Yes. The standard package includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. If you want champagne, cocktails, or a catering upgrade, let us know on WhatsApp and we will quote accordingly.

How many people can join the sunset photoshoot?

The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 carry up to 9 and 11 passengers respectively. The Mangusta 80 can take up to 12. For smaller groups, the Speedboat (6 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are also options.

What happens if the weather is bad on the day?

We monitor conditions closely. If the captain deems it unsafe, we will reschedule at no extra cost or offer a full refund. For light drizzle or cloud cover, the shoot can still go ahead – overcast light can be flattering for portraits.

Ready to book your Marbella sunset photoshoot yacht? Browse our full fleet or send us a WhatsApp message to check availability for your preferred date. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during July and August.
